title: do_op
subject:
Write a program that takes three arguments: an integer, an operator, and another integer. It applies the operator to the two integers (using integer arithmetic) and prints the result followed by a newline. The operator is one of + - * / %.

If the number of arguments is not exactly 3, print just a newline. Use atoi-style parsing for the numbers.

Examples:
  $ ./a.out 3 + 4
  7
  $ ./a.out 10 / 3
  3
  $ ./a.out 6 "*" 7
  42
